title: 第 50 節
en: The same applies to the names of heroes, they also are shortened, and receive new suffixes in their hypokoristic employment. Thus Herakles is called Heryllos, Heraios, Herykalos ; Iphianassa, Iphis ;
1 See Fick and Bechtel, Griech. Personennamen, pp. 16-36. Some of these so-called hypokoristic names are dialectic and ancient.
368 PROPER NAMES OF GODS AND HEROES. [chap.
Amphiaraos, Amphis ; Bellerophontes, Belleros ; Atrometos, Tromes.
Nor are the names of gods exempt from this treatment. We find what may be called coaxing names such as Demo for Demeter, Eleutho for Eleuthyia, Aphro for Aphrogeneia, Trito for Tritogeneia.
Dialectic varieties of Proper Names.

In some of these cases, however, it is very doubtful whether the coaxing name is really a shortening or modification of the fuller name, and not rather a parallel form of independent origin. Admitting that Demo is a shortening of Demeter, we can hardly say the same of Deo, which may be on the contrary a name like Jovis by the side of Jupiter, may in fact be a feminine form of Dyaus corresponding to the dual Dyava, in the Sanskrit Dyava-pWthivi1. Erechtheus and Erichthonios look like parallel dialectic varieties, and do not necessitate the admission that Erechtheus was the shortened hypokoristic form of Erichthonios.

Nor do I see that much would have been gained if we supposed that Hermas, Hermaon, and the Thessal. Hermauos had been shortened on purpose from Hermeias. These names are far more naturally explained as dialectic varieties, quite as much as the Aeol. Poseidan,the Ion. Poseideon, the Arkad.Posoidan,the Thessal. Poteidoun, the Lak. Pohoidan, by the side of the Att. Poseidon. The same applies to other varieties such as Posoida, Poteida, Potida, &c. Why should Kypris be taken as a secondary form as compared with Kyprogeneia, or Aphro as compared with Aphrodite ?

v] DIALECTIC VARIETIES OF PROPER NAMES. 369 Hekate does not seem to presuppose Hekatebolos, nor Phersis, Phersephone, nor Aello, Aellopous. No one would feel inclined to explain Jason ('idcrajv) as a shortening of Jasilaos, Jasidemos, or any other compound beginning with Iolctl, in the sense of healing. What these names really teach us is that the gods, being worshipped in different localities, their names, far more than any ordinary words, often preserved their local dialectic colouring. Before we analyse and compare mythological names, we ought to remember how often, particularly in Sanskrit, different suffixes are used after one and the same root, to form substantives of exactly or very nearly the same meaning. From the root sar, to move along, for instance, we find sar-iman and sariman, sar-a£, sar-a^a, sar-ati, and sar-awyu, all meaning wind; sar-ani is a path, sar-ma means going; sar-it is a river, Sar-ayu is the name of a river. Can we doubt then that Sar-awyii, wind, or morning-wind, is but a parallel form of Sar-ama, and that if the Greek opfjLT], impetus, German Sturm, corresponds to Sk. sarma, as a fern., it corresponds equally well to Sarama, the mother of the Sarameya twins ?

There is always something sacred about divine names, and we can well understand that Greeks speaking different dialects in their various settlements, should retain the names most familiar to them when speaking of their gods. If we keep this in mind we shall be better able to understand the anomalies in the names of many of the Greek gods. Hermes may presuppose the fuller form Hermeias, but Hermaon, Herman, and Hermauos are clearly names formed independently, though all from the same stem, which we have in opfx-q, in Sk. Sarama vol. 1. b b 370 DIALECTIC VARIETIES OF PROPER NAMES. [chap.
and her offspring Sarameya1, to my mind one of the best established facts in Comparative Mythology, and worthy of its first discoverer and patron, Adalbert Kuhn.

It is generally admitted that Eileithyia is a name of the same deity as Eleuthyia and Eleutho, but how ei can replace eu, or vice versa, has never been explained. Facts, however, are facts, whether we can explain them or not, and not even the most determined sceptic would deny that Apollon, Apellon, and Aploun are dialectic varieties of the same name, whatever outcry might be raised against similar changes in other words. We should also take into account a peculiarity in the formation of proper names in Sanskrit, to which I have called attention before. We are generally inclined to look upon a proper name as something settled once for all, as a mark that cannot be altered without losing its character, as something no longer significative, but purely indicative. But that is not the case, least of all in Sanskrit. Here Ziandrasena, /iandradatta are as good as ^Tandragupta. We have a strikingexample of this in the name of Buddha's wife. She is called Yasodhant, i.e. glory-bearing, in Pali, and likewise in Sanskrit, but by the side of this name we also find Yaso-vati, glorious, while Yaso-da, glorygiving, is the name of the wife of Mahavira among the Camas 2. And what we see in India, even in the case of living persons, we see again and again in the names of Greek mythology. It makes no 1 As to the elision of the middle vowel, see Harpyiae and Arepyiae, Fick, Griech. Personennamen, p. 467.
2 Senart, Legende du Buddha, p. 306.

v] DIALECTIC VARIETIES OF PROPER NAMES. 37 1 difference whether the mother of Jason, for instance, is called Polymele or Polymede or Polypheme. Nay, she is recognised even under other names, such as Alkimede and Amphinome, to say nothing of quite independent names, such as Arne, Skarphe, and Bhoio. Instead of Eurynome, the mother of the Charites, we find Eurymedousa, Eunomia and several other names 1. All these are facts that cannot be ignored.

But if I mention these and similar anomalies, it is not in order to place the comparison and etymologies of mythological names completely beyond the reach of phonetic laws. Far from it. But at the same time laws cannot supersede facts, and the anomalous changes in the names of ancient deities should not be ignored by any conscientious student. If facts teach us that it is exceptional for proper and local names to follow the same phonetic rules as appellative nouns, the cases in which the proper names of gods and heroes are changed in strict accordance with phonetic laws should be looked upon as fortunate rather than as what we have a right to expect. Dialectic peculiarities, if carefully studied, are much more likely to throw light on the varieties of mythological names than the universal phonetic rules derived from the classical languages whether of the Aryan or the Semitic family.

With all this there is little danger of our drifting back into the etymologies of mythological names that were favoured by ancient Greek grammarians, or are even now seriously put forward by classical scholars, to say nothing of the wild guesses of 1 Gerhard, Griech. Mythologie, s. v. B b 2 372 DIALECTIC VARIETIES OF PROPER NAMES. [chap.

Semitic scholars in the seventeenth and again in the nineteenth century. We shall hardly hear again of Persians being derived from Perseus, Medians from Medeia, or Ionians from Ion. Nor is Apollon likely to be explained once more as the destroyer, from airoWvvai, because Aeschylus in the Agamemnon, v. 1 080, said : —
"AnoXkov AttoXXov ayviar anoXXuv ep.os.
dn'oiXecraj yap ov po\is to (jevrepov.
The ancients may be pardoned for an etymology of Helena such as we find in the Agamemnon of Aeschylus, v. 681 : —
Ti's ttot uivojia^ev a>h (S TO TTdV CTT)TVpG)i pi] tis ovtw ov)( 6pu>- ptv TTpovoiaio-i tov neTrpoopevov yXaxjcrav iv TV^a vepcov ; —
rav hopiyapfipov ap(piv(iK.r) 6' EXfVttv TTii TTptTIOVTOIS eXevas, eXavSpos, iXenroXis. . . .
New Etymologies by Professor Bechtel. Dionysos.

The ancient Greeks did not hesitate to derive the name of Dionysos from his father (Zeus, Aid?) and his supposed birthplace Nuo-77, though what the meaning of such a compound could have been, is difficult to say. But I do not think we are much better off when one of the most recent etymologists, Prof. Bechtel, derives the same name from Dios and snutya. What is such a compound to mean ? Snu in Sanskrit means to run, to flow, and it is represented in Greek by w, which appears in vioi, evevcra, &c. Sniita in Sanskrit would mean running, possibly a stream, and snutya might be an v] dionysos. 373 adjective of snuta. As snu is often used with reference to mother milk, snuta might be interpreted as such, and snutya as fed on mother milk. This might lend some weight to Corssen's etymology of nutrix or snutrix. But could Dionysos ever have been called the nursling or suckling of Zeus 1 Zeus has performed many miracles, he actually became, for a time at least, the parent of Dionysos (fjL7]porpa(f)7]^, but he never was conceived as giving the breast to this wonderful suckling. Bechtel therefore proposes to take Dionysos as originally a form of Zeus whose name he bears in the first part of his name. The second part is then derived by him from vclFoj (snavo) to flow. The god, he argues, was called ' the flow of the sky or of light,' and is fundamentally the same as Zevs Ncuos of Dodona, surrounded by the NafiaSe?.

Admitting that snutya in Greek could mean flow, or stream, sky-stream would be a strange name for Dionysos, and his identity with his father or with the source whence he is supposed to flow forth, is again a strong demand on our faith, or rather on our credulity. But what about the phonetic rules ? First of all, though the meaning of vdco and via) is the same, it would be well to keep the two roots apart, as they are kept apart by Curtius, and likewise in Sanskrit in snu and sna. Thus Curtius derives votlos, moist, from sna, not from snu, and likewise m/xa, moisture, N^peus, &c. Secondly, nothing could be more regular than the change of v into ef and ev, as in via), veva), and vevcns. But Prof. Bechtel says nothing to account for the long v of Dionysos, though he is aware that this long v is anomalous, and cannot be matched by any other 374 DIONYSOS. [CHAP.

derivative either of sna or snu. If his etymology supplied a really successful explanation of the character of Dionysos, this lengthening of the vowel might possibly be condoned, but the suckling of Zeus can hardly claim such indulgence.
Kerberos.

Classical writers had not much to say as to the etymology of Kerberos, still the statement of Aristarchos at Odyss. xi, 14, that there was for Kt/x/xeptot another reading Kepfiepcoi, did serve as a useful hint, and led Liddell and Scott in their Greek Dictionary to suggest Darkling as the original meaning of Kepfiepos.

Bechtel, however, who is not generally enamoured of mythological etymologies, declares positively that Kepfiepos belongs to a root Kepfi = serg, to be stiff, from which also Kopvufios, the uppermost point or head, KpajfivXos, tuft of hair, and Sk. -srmga, horn. Supposing that epos in KoXepos stands for epos, wool, he takes Kepfiepos for stiff-woolled. Admitting that Kerberos or dogs in general had wool instead of hair, koXo + Fepos would surely become KoXoFepos and KoXovpos (Brugmann, vol. ii, p. 45), rather than KoXepos.
zh: 同樣的情況也適用於英雄的名字，這些名字在昵稱用法中同樣會被縮短，並接受新的後綴。因此赫拉克勒斯被稱為赫律洛斯、赫拉奧斯、赫律卡洛斯；伊菲亞納莎被稱為伊菲斯；
參見 Fick 與 Bechtel《希臘人名》第 16-36 頁。某些所謂的昵稱形式具有方言特色，且年代久遠。
368 神祇與英雄的專有名詞 [章節
安菲亞拉俄斯（Amphiaraos）、安菲斯（Amphis）；貝勒洛豐忒斯（Bellerophontes）、貝勒洛斯（Belleros）；阿特洛美托斯（Atrometos）、特洛美斯（Tromes）。
諸神的名字也不能逃脫這種處理。我們發現了所謂的「哄騙式名字」，如 Demo 源於 Demeter、Eleutho 源於 Eleuthyia、Aphro 源於 Aphrogeneia、Trito 源於 Tritogeneia。
專有名詞的辯證變體

在這些例子中，有些情況很值得懷疑，所謂的昵稱（coaxing name）是否真的是完整名字的縮短或變化形式，還是不如說本身就是獨立起源的平行形式。倘若我們承認 Demo 是 Demeter 的縮短形，那麼對於 Deo 便難以做同樣的說法，Deo 反而可能是如 Jovis 相對於 Jupiter 一類的名字，實際上可能是 Dyaus 的陰性形式，對應梵文中的雙數形 Dyava-pṛthivī¹。Erechtheus 和 Erichthonios 看起來像是平行的方言變體，並不需要我們承認 Erechtheus 是 Erichthonios 的縮短昵稱形式。

我認為即使假設赫爾瑪斯（Hermas）、赫爾邁翁（Hermaon）與塞薩利方言的赫爾毛俄斯（Thessal. Hermauos）乃是有意從赫爾梅亞斯（Hermeias）縮略而來，也不會獲得太多益處。這些名稱遠為自然地解釋為方言變體，恰如埃奧利亞方言的波塞達（Aeol. Poseidan）、愛奧尼亞方言的波塞德翁（Ion. Poseideon）、阿卡狄亞方言的波索達（Arkad. Posoidan）、塞薩利方言的波特德翁（Thessal. Poteidoun）、拉克尼亞方言的波霍達（Lak. Pohoidan），與雅典方言的波塞冬（Att. Poseidon）相並列。同樣的情況亦適用於其他變體，如波索達（Posoida）、波特達（Poteida）、波提達（Potida）等等。何以庫普裡絲（Kypris）應當被視為相較於庫普羅革尼亞（Kyprogeneia）的次級形式，或阿夫羅（Aphro）相較於阿佛羅狄忒（Aphrodite）的次級形式？

我注意到您提供的「英文原文」只是一則腳注引用：「1 L- c., p. 376.」 這是學術文獻的縮寫形式，意為： - **1** = 腳注編號 - **L- c.** = *loco citato*（拉丁文，意為「在上述位置」） - **p. 376** = 第 376 頁 此類引用標記本身不需翻譯，在繁體中文學術著作中通常保持原樣。若您需要我翻譯穆勒《宗教學導論》的具體內容段落，請提供完整的英文原文，我將按照規則進行翻譯。

赫卡忒（Hekate）似乎不必然預設赫卡忒博洛斯（Hekatebolos），斐爾西絲（Phersis）不必然預設斐爾塞芬妮（Phersephone），埃洛（Aello）不必然預設埃洛普斯（Aellopous）。沒有人會傾向於將傑生（Jason，ἴασον）解釋為傑西勞斯（Jasilaos）、傑西德摩斯（Jasidemos）或任何其他以「治癒」之意開始的複合詞的縮略形式。這些名字真正教導我們的是：眾神在不同的地方受到崇拜，他們的名字遠比普通詞彙更經常保留其地方方言的色彩。在我們分析和比較神話名字之前，應該記得在梵文中尤其常見的現象——從同一個詞根衍生出不同的字尾，以形成意義完全相同或極為相似的名詞。例如從詞根 sar（沿著移動）出發，我們發現 sar-iman 和 sariman、sar-aċ、sar-a^a、sar-ati 和 sar-awyu 都表示風；sar-ani 是路徑，sar-ma 表示行進；sar-it 是河流，Sar-ayu 是一條河的名稱。那麼我們豈能懷疑 Sar-awyu（風或晨風）不過是 Sar-ama 的並行形式，且若希臘語 ὁρμή（衝動）、德語 Sturm 對應於梵文 sarma（陰性），它也同樣對應於薩羅摩（Sarama），即薩羅美亞雙胞胎的母親？

神聖的名字總是帶有某種神聖性，我們完全可以理解，在各自定居點講著不同方言的希臘人在談論他們的諸神時，應該保留對他們而言最為熟悉的名字。如果我們記住這一點，就能更好地理解許多希臘諸神名字中的異常現象。赫爾墨斯（Hermes）可能預設了更完整的形式赫爾梅亞斯（Hermeias），但赫爾瑪翁（Hermaon）、赫爾曼（Herman）和赫爾毛俄斯（Hermauos）顯然是獨立形成的名字，儘管它們都源於同一個詞根，我們在希臘文 opfx-q 及梵文（Sk.）莎羅摩（Sarama）中可以看到這個詞根。
及其後裔莎拉梅亞（Sarameya），在我看來，這是比較神話學中最有根據的事實之一，值得推崇其首位發現者與倡導者阿達爾貝特‧庫恩（Adalbert Kuhn）。

一般公認 Eileithyia 與 Eleuthyia 和 Eleutho 是同一尊神祇的名稱，但 ei 如何替代 eu，或反之亦然，始終未曾得到解釋。然而，事實就是事實，無論我們能否解釋之，即使最堅決的懷疑論者也不會否認 Apollon、Apellon 和 Aploun 是同一名稱的方言變體，無論對其他詞彙中類似的變化提出何等異議。我們也應當考慮梵語專有名詞構成的一項特殊性，我在此前業已提及。我們通常傾向於將專有名詞視為某種一成不變之物，視為無法改變而不失其特性的標記，視為不再具有意義而純粹具指示性的東西。然而事實並非如此，尤其在梵語中更非如此。在梵語中，Ziandrasena、Jiandradatta 同樣有效於 Chandragupta。我們可從佛陀妻子的名稱中得到一個引人注目的例子。她在巴利語中被稱為 Yasodhara，即「榮光承載者」之意，在梵語中亦然，但除此名稱外，我們還發現 Yaso-vati，意為「光榮的」，而 Yaso-da，意為「光榮授予者」，則是耆那教中大雄（Mahavira）妻子的名稱。我們在印度所見者，即使在活人的名稱上，我們在希臘神話的名稱中也一再見到。此毫無1 --- 1 關於中間母音的省略，見 Harpyiae 和 Arepyiae，Fick，《希臘人名》，第 467 頁。
2 塞納爾，《佛陀傳說》，第306頁。

在傑森的母親是稱為波呂墨勒（Polymele）、波呂墨得（Polymede）或波呂斐墨（Polypheme）之間，實在沒有什麼分別。不僅如此，她甚至還被認可使用其他名字，例如阿爾基墨得（Alkimede）和安菲諾墨（Amphinome），更不用說那些完全獨立的名字，如阿涅（Arne）、斯卡爾斐（Skarphe）和波奧（Bhoio）。關於恩里諾墨（Eurynome），即美惠三女神的母親，我們發現她也被稱為歐里墨多烏薩（Eurymedousa）、歐諾彌亞（Eunomia）以及其他幾個名字¹。所有這些都是不容忽視的事實。

但我若提及這些及類似的異常現象，並非意在將神話名稱的比較與詞源完全置於音韻規律的掌握之外。絕非如此。然而同時音韻規律也不能取代事實，而古代神祇名稱中的異常變化不應為任何具有良知的學者所忽視。倘若事實教導我們，專名與地名遵循與普通名詞相同音韻規律乃是特例，那麼諸神與英雄的專名能夠嚴格按照音韻規律而變化的情況，應當被視為幸運之舉，而非我們有權預期的結果。方言特性若經仔細研究，遠比源自古典語言（無論雅利安或閃語族語言）的通用音韻規律更有可能為神話名稱的多樣性提供啟蒙。
有了這一切，我們幾乎不會有滑回古代希臘語法學家所偏好的神話名稱詞源學的危險，也不會陷入當代古典學者仍在認真提出的那些詮釋，更不用說那些荒謬的猜測了。¹ ¹ Gerhard, *Griech. Mythologie*, s. v. B b --- **第 372 節 專有名詞的方言變異**
閃語族學者在十七世紀再次在十九世紀所為。我們不太可能再聽聞波斯人源自珀爾修斯、米底亞人源自美狄亞、或愛奧尼亞人源自埃翁的說法。阿波羅也不太可能再被解釋為「毀滅者」，來自 airoWvvai，僅因為埃斯庫羅斯在《阿伽門農》第一〇八〇行所言：——
我注意到您提供的英文原文只包含希臘文字（「ἀπόλλων Ἀπόλλων ἁγνίας ἀπόλλυν ἔρος」），而非英文。 根據您的規則第2條，我應僅翻譯英文原文，此處並無英文內容可供翻譯。 請確認是否需要提供完整的英文原文，以便我進行翻譯。
我注意到您提供的「英文原文」實際上是亂碼或加密文字，而非可讀的英文內容。 請提供正確的英文原文，我將按照您的規則進行翻譯。
古人對海倫娜（Helena）的語源學解釋可以得到原諒，正如我們在埃斯庫羅斯（Aeschylus）的《阿伽門農》（Agamemnon）第 681 行所見的那樣：
我注意到您提供的「英文原文」實際上是希臘文字符，而非英文。根據翻譯規則第2條，我只能翻譯英文原文。 請提供正確的英文原文，我將按照您的規則進行翻譯。 若您想翻譯這段希臘文，請另行說明，但這超出了目前的委託範圍。
我注意到您提供的「英文原文」實際上是古希臘文字符，而非英文。根據您的翻譯規則第2條，我應該只翻譯英文原文。 請您提供正確的英文原文，我將按照您的規則進行繁體中文翻譯。 如果您想翻譯上述古希臘文內容，請另行說明，我可以協助，但這將超出「英文原文翻譯」的範圍。
貝希特爾教授的新詞源學。狄俄尼索斯。

古代希臘人毫不猶豫地從狄俄尼索斯（Dionysos）的父親（宙斯，Zeus）和他名義上的出生地紐歐（Nuo-77）推導其名字的由來，儘管這樣的複合詞究竟具有何種含義實難說清。但當最近期的語言學家之一貝希特爾教授從「狄歐斯」（Dios）和「斯努提亞」（snutya）推導同一個名字時，我認為我們的情況也好不到哪裡去。這樣的複合詞又該作何解呢？梵文中的 snu 意為「奔流」、「流動」，在希臘文中由 w 代表，此字母出現於 νιοί、ἐνέχετρα 等詞彙中。梵文中的 snita 會意為「奔流」、「或許是一股水流」，而 snutya 可能是 snuta 的一個形容詞。既然 snu 常與母乳有關，snuta 或可解釋為母乳，snutya 則為「靠母乳哺育」。這種解釋或許可為科爾森關於 nutrix 或 snutrix 的語源學說增加若干分量。但狄俄尼索斯豈能曾被稱為宙斯的哺兒或吮兒乎？宙斯誠然行過許多奇蹟，他實際上在某個時期成為了狄俄尼索斯的生父（ὀρροφαφία），然而他從未被設想為給予這位奇異吮兒乳汁。因此貝希特爾主張將狄俄尼索斯視為最初乃是宙斯的一種形式，其名字的前半部分即宙斯之名。後半部分則由他從 ϝέλϝος（snavo）「流動」推導而出。他辯稱，這位神靈被稱為「天空或光明之流」，本質上與陶多那的宙斯涅歐斯（Zevs Neaos）相同，周圍環繞著納依阿得斯（Nαϊάδες）。

承認在希臘文中 snutya 可以表示「流動」或「溪流」，「天空溪流」作為狄俄尼索斯的名字實屬奇怪，而他與其父親或他所據稱流出之源頭的同一性，再次對我們的信念—或者說對我們的輕信—提出了強烈的要求。但音韻規律又如何呢？首先，雖然 vdco（以及 via）的意義相同，但最好將這兩個詞根分開，如柯蒂烏斯所為，同樣在梵文中 snu 和 sna 也應分開。因此柯蒂烏斯將 votlos（濕潤的）從 sna 推導而來，而非從 snu；同樣也推導 m/xa（濕氣）、N^peus 等。其次，v 變為 ef 和 ev 的轉變再規則不過了，如在 via）、veva）和 vevcns 中所見。但貝希特爾教授對於狄俄尼索斯長音 v 的出現沒有做任何說明，儘管他察覺到這個長音 v 是異常的，無法與任何其他例子相匹配。
衍生自 sna 或 snu。若其詞源學能夠真正成功地解釋狄俄尼索斯（Dionysos）的特性，這種元音的延長或許尚可原諒，但宙斯的哺育之說實在難以獲得如此寬恕。
刻耳柏洛斯（Kerberos）。
古典作家對刻耳柏洛斯（Kerberos）的字源學著墨不多，但阿里斯塔科斯（Aristarchos）在《奧德賽》第十一卷第十四行的陳述確實提供了有用的線索——他指出 Κτ/χ/χερτοι 還有另一個讀法 Κεφθερ­σοι——這引導利德爾和斯科特在他們的《希臘語詞典》中提議「暗黑」（Darkling）作為 Κεφθερος 的原始含義。

然而，貝希特爾（Bechtel）雖然通常不甚贊同神話學的詞源學，但明確宣稱 Κέρϐερος 屬於詞根 Κέρϐ = σέργ（僵硬、變硬），同一詞根也產生了 Κορυϕαῖος（最高處或頭部）、Κρανίαλος（髮束）及梵文 sṛṅga（角）。假設 εpos 在 Κολέρος 中代表 εpos（毛髮）意義，他認為 Κέρϐερος 意為「毛髮僵硬的」。但即使承認刻耳柏洛斯或一般的狗具有毛髮而非獸毛，κολο + ϝερος 必然應該變成 κολοϝερος 和 κολουρος（布魯格曼，第二卷，第 45 頁），而非 κολερος。
